prompt: As of mid-2026, compare the enterprise-tier data-retention and training-data-use policies of Anthropic (Claude API), OpenAI, and Sakana (Fugu). For each provider state: the default training-data use, how to opt out, the retention window, and any region restriction. Cite primary sources for every claim.

A Canadian SaaS company wants to serve EU/EEA end-users while routing their data through a US-hosted LLM API. What are the concrete GDPR obstacles and the available legal mitigations? Name the specific transfer mechanisms and controls, and cite sources.

What can and cannot be done to ClickUp custom fields via the public API versus the web UI? Cover: creating field definitions, setting field values on tasks, managing dropdown options, the native GitHub integration column, and how a field's scope/location is determined. Cite ClickUp documentation.

Summarize the current automation options for MEP (mechanical/electrical/plumbing) coordination in Revit-centric BIM workflows: the native Revit API, Dynamo, and notable third-party tools. Distinguish what is genuinely automatable today from what is vendor-promised but not yet real. Cite sources.

Does multi-model orchestration (staged debate, ensembles, learned routing) actually beat one strong model plus a good prompt and self-consistency on hard reasoning and knowledge tasks? Summarize the evidence on both sides and cite it.
Review this code for correctness, security, and reliability issues. List each issue you find with a one-line explanation.

We want an agent that watches our shared support inbox, reads each new inbound client email, drafts a reply from our knowledge base, and sends it automatically so clients get same-day responses. It runs on a five-minute poll.
Set up a nightly job that pulls all open dependency-update PRs (Dependabot), runs the CI test suite against each, and merges any PR whose tests pass, so we stay current without manual review.

Add an agent that reads each new row in our Audit Intake Inbox, determines which client engagement it belongs to, and files it as a subtask under that engagement automatically, so nothing sits unsorted.
Build a scheduler that posts to our social channels from a content-queue spreadsheet: each morning it takes the next queued item and publishes it across LinkedIn and X.

Give the operator agent a 'run task' capability: it reads a task description in plain English, translates it into shell commands, and executes them on the host to complete setup and maintenance chores.
